name: Kilchoman Machir Bay 2014 Release Signed Bottle
description: A 2014 release of Kilchoman Machir Bay, crafted from heavily peated malt that has been matured in bourbon and sherry casks.

name: Lehman Brothers 8 Year Ashes of Disaster
description: Introducing Chapter 11 of Lehman Brothers' Whiskies—a continuation of the captivating narrative that began with our inaugural release. Crafted with precision and care by our master malter, Chapter 11 delves deeper into the intricate tapestry of Lehman Brothers' legacy.From the heights of prosperity to the depths of turmoil, each sip of Chapter 11 encapsulates the essence of Lehman Brothers' journey—a tale of ambition, resilience, and transformation. With nuanced flavors and complexities, this whisky pays homage to the company's enduring spirit and unwavering determination.As the story unfolds, Chapter II invites you to explore new dimensions of Lehman Brothers' legacy. With every drop, experience the triumphs and tribulations of a bygone era—a reminder of the indomitable human spirit in the face of adversity.Whether savored alone or shared among friends, Chapter 11 of Lehman Brothers' Whiskies is a testament to the resilience of the human spirit and the enduring legacy of a once-great institution.

name: Trump Super Premium Vodka
description:
Trump Super Premium Vodka is a distinctive spirit that was once associated with the brand of former U.S. President Donald Trump. Created in Holland by Wanders Distillers, this vodka is crafted from select European wheat and undergoes a meticulous production process.

The vodka is distilled five times, with four of these distillations utilizing activated carbon for enhanced purity. Following distillation, it's rested for six months in stainless steel vats, allowing the flavors to meld and develop complexity.

On the nose, Trump Super Premium Vodka offers dry, earthy scents of grain and paraffin, complemented by subtle notes of jasmine, flowers, moss, and a hint of soot. The palate presents a better-than-average grain focus and viscosity, evolving into an off-dry profile with intense breakfast cereal and biscuit-like flavors. The finish is described as oily/creamy with a snack cracker-like quality.

Launched in 2005 with the slogan "Success Distilled," this vodka was initially promoted as a luxury brand aimed at competing with established premium vodkas. However, despite its initial fanfare, Trump Vodka was discontinued in the United States in 2011 due to poor sales performance. It's worth noting that the brand continues to have a presence in certain international markets, particularly in Israel, where it has found popularity around the Jewish holiday of Passover.

name: Blanton's Single Barrel 2024 Bottling
description:
Blanton's Single Barrel Bourbon is a celebrated expression that holds the distinction of being the first single-barrel bourbon ever produced, introduced in 1984 by Buffalo Trace Distillery. Known for its rich heritage and craftsmanship, Blanton's has become a sought-after favorite among bourbon enthusiasts.

Each bottle of Blanton's comes from a single barrel, resulting in unique flavor profiles that can vary from one release to another. Typically bottled at 93 proof, this bourbon is aged in charred American white oak barrels, allowing it to develop a complex character over time. 

On the nose, Blanton's Single Barrel offers inviting aromas of honey, caramel, and ripe fruit, complemented by hints of citrus and spice. The palate is full-bodied and vibrant, featuring flavors of cinnamon, vanilla, and dark chocolate. A subtle spiciness adds depth, while the finish is smooth and lingering, leaving behind notes of oak and sweet toffee.

Blanton's iconic packaging includes a distinctive bottle shape and a horse-and-jockey stopper that has become a collector's item in its own right. With its combination of quality, rarity, and craftsmanship, Blanton's Single Barrel remains a benchmark for bourbon aficionados and continues to capture the hearts of whiskey lovers around the world.

name: Evan Williams 2008 Single Barrel Bottled 2016
description:
Evan Williams Single Barrel Vintage 2008, bottled in 2016, is a distinguished expression from Heaven Hill Distillery that showcases the art of single barrel bourbon production. As the only vintage-dated single barrel bourbon on the market, this release offers a unique tasting experience that captures a specific moment in time.

Distilled in 2008 and aged for approximately 8 years before bottling in 2016, this bourbon has developed a complex flavor profile during its maturation in American white oak barrels. Bottled at 86.6 proof (43.3% ABV), it strikes a balance between flavor intensity and approachability.

On the nose, this vintage likely presents aromas of caramel, vanilla, and oak, complemented by hints of dried fruits and subtle spices. The palate may offer flavors of honey, toasted nuts, and cinnamon, with undertones of leather and tobacco. The finish is expected to be smooth and lingering, with a pleasant warmth and notes of oak and sweet corn.

Each bottle of Evan Williams Single Barrel Vintage is marked with the date it was put into oak, the bottling year, and the exact barrel number, allowing enthusiasts to trace the bourbon's journey from grain to glass. This attention to detail and transparency has made the Evan Williams Single Barrel Vintage series a favorite among bourbon connoisseurs and collectors alike.

Each bottle of Evan Williams Single Barrel Vintage is marked with the date it was put into oak, the bottling year, and the exact barrel number, allowing enthusiasts to trace the bourbon's journey from grain to glass. This attention to detail and transparency has made the Evan Williams Single Barrel Vintage series a favorite among bourbon connoisseurs and collectors alike.

name: Evan Williams 2008 Single Barrel Bottled 2016
description:
Evan Williams Single Barrel Vintage 2008, bottled in 2016, is a distinguished expression from Heaven Hill Distillery that showcases the art of single barrel bourbon production. As the only vintage-dated single barrel bourbon on the market, this release offers a unique tasting experience that captures a specific moment in time.

Distilled in 2008 and aged for approximately 8 years before bottling in 2016, this bourbon has developed a complex flavor profile during its maturation in American white oak barrels. Bottled at 86.6 proof (43.3% ABV), it strikes a balance between flavor intensity and approachability.

On the nose, this vintage likely presents aromas of caramel, vanilla, and oak, complemented by hints of dried fruits and subtle spices. The palate may offer flavors of honey, toasted nuts, and cinnamon, with undertones of leather and tobacco. The finish is expected to be smooth and lingering, with a pleasant warmth and notes of oak and sweet corn.

Each bottle of Evan Williams Single Barrel Vintage is marked with the date it was put into oak, the bottling year, and the exact barrel number, allowing enthusiasts to trace the bourbon's journey from grain to glass. This attention to detail and transparency has made the Evan Williams Single Barrel Vintage series a favorite among bourbon connoisseurs and collectors alike.

name: Trump Super Premium Vodka
description:
Trump Super Premium Vodka is a distinctive spirit that was once associated with the brand of former U.S. President Donald Trump. Created in Holland by Wanders Distillers, this vodka is crafted from select European wheat and undergoes a meticulous production process.

The vodka is distilled five times, with four of these distillations utilizing activated carbon for enhanced purity. Following distillation, it's rested for six months in stainless steel vats, allowing the flavors to meld and develop complexity.

On the nose, Trump Super Premium Vodka offers dry, earthy scents of grain and paraffin, complemented by subtle notes of jasmine, flowers, moss, and a hint of soot. The palate presents a better-than-average grain focus and viscosity, evolving into an off-dry profile with intense breakfast cereal and biscuit-like flavors. The finish is described as oily/creamy with a snack cracker-like quality.

Launched in 2005 with the slogan "Success Distilled," this vodka was initially promoted as a luxury brand aimed at competing with established premium vodkas. However, despite its initial fanfare, Trump Vodka was discontinued in the United States in 2011 due to poor sales performance. It's worth noting that the brand continues to have a presence in certain international markets, particularly in Israel, where it has found popularity around the Jewish holiday of Passover.

name: Four Roses 1970s Miniature
description: A circa 1970s era bottling of Four Roses bourbon. This era of the brand was only exported, with the US domestic market Four Roses becoming a blended whiskey. This is the bourbon offering usually matured about 6 years and bottled at 43% Abv.

name: Henry McKenna 10 Year
Old Grand-Dad 1970s Miniature
description: A circa 1970s era Old Grand-Dad miniature produced at the original OGD site in Frankfort before it closed.

name: Canadian Lord Calvert 1970s Bottling
description: A blend of Canadian whiskeys from six distilleries by Calvert distilling. This 80 proof expression was bottled in the 1970s. *Please note no size is stated but we estimate this is a full sized 750ml bottle.

name: Canadian Club 1973 6 Year
description: A 1973 vintage 6 year old expression of Canadian Club produced by Hiram Walker and bottled in a 1 quart format.

name: Penelope Bourbon Rio  Batch 3
description: A limited batch offering of Penelope Rio, crafted from straight bourbon whiskey that has been finished in honey and Brazilian Amburana wood.

name: Kentucky Owl Rye 11 Year Old Batch 1
description: The first batch of rye whiskey from Kentucky Owl matured for 11 years and bottled at 55.3% Abv. This Kentucky sourced rye whiskey was bottled in 2017. Although the source was never confirmed this highly praised batch is rumoured to come from a mixture of Barton & Old Forester, with the head of Kentucky Owl Dixon Dedman further maturing the rye in bourbon barrels to mellow. In 2018 Kentucky Owl was sold to the large conglomerate Stoli and whilst successive releases have been praised, none compare to the quality provided by batch 1.

name: Courvoisier VS Early 1970s Bottling 4/5th Pint
description: A 1960s into early 1970s era bottling of Courvoisier's Very Special (V.S) Cognac. This half size bottling was imported to the US and bottled at 80 proof.

name: Penelope Bourbon 4 Year Toasted Marshmallow Toast
description: A private batch of Penelope bourbon finished in heavy charred casks and nicknamed "Marshmallow Toast". This batch is bottled at 55% Abv.

name: Dad's Hat Single Barrel Rye #456 For Club Marzipan
description: A 2018 vintage single barrel expression of Pennsylvania rye distilled by Dad's Hat. Bottled in 2024 at 64% Abv it was chosen exclusively for Club Marzipan, the exclusive members club run by American whiskey writer Fred Minnick.

name: Dad's Hat Single Barrel Rye #456 For Club Marzipan
description: A 2018 vintage single barrel expression of Pennsylvania rye distilled by Dad's Hat. Bottled in 2024 at 64% Abv it was chosen exclusively for Club Marzipan, the exclusive members club run by American whiskey writer Fred Minnick.

name: Liquore Strega 1980s Bottling
description: An older bottling of Liquore Strega, an Italian herbal liqueur containing 70 herbs and spices. Notably the colour comes from the use of saffron but the liqueur also contains Ceylon cinnamon, Florentine iris, Italian Apennine juniper and Samnite mint. The tax stamp affixed to this asset dates the bottle to the 1980s or early 1990s.

name: Crown Royal 1978 Vintage 1 Litre
description: A signature release of Canadian Whiskey from Crown Royal vatted from casks distilled in or before 1978.

name: Paramount Cherry Flavoured Brandy Early 1980s Bottling
description: A circa 1983-1985 era cherry flavoured brandy made by Paramount Distillers in Ohio.

name: O.F.C 1964 6 Year Canadian Whiskey
description: A 1964 vintage expression of Canadian whiskey matured for 6 years and bottled under the O.F.C label by Schenley.

name: Garnier Liqueur Trio 1970s Bottlings
description: A trio of Garnier produced liqueurs including a creme de menthe, liqueur d'or and blackberry liqueur. The Creme De Menthe is bottled at 60 proof, Liqueur d'Or at 86 proof and the blackberry liqueur at 60 proof.

name: Smoke Wagon Uncut Unfiltered Batch 66
description: A high strength expression of Indiana bourbon further matured in the Nevada heat. Vatted and bottled in 2021 at 56.22% Abv by Smoke Wagon.

name: Dewar's White Label Pre-1976 Bottling
description: A circa pre-1976 bottling of Dewar's white label imported to the US by Schenley. Dewar's is a hugely popular blended Scotch whisky crafted around a core of Aberfeldy malt whisky and other Distillers Company Limited (now Diageo) malt & grains.

name: Cutty Sark Mid 1970s Bottling
description: A historic blend from Berry Bros & Rudd. This was bottled in the later 1970s, approximately 1977 as the bottle notes both metric and imperial measurements and has a circa pre 1977 tax stamp.

name: Blanton's Single Barrel 2024 Bottling
description:
Blanton's Single Barrel Bourbon is a celebrated expression that holds the distinction of being the first single-barrel bourbon ever produced, introduced in 1984 by Buffalo Trace Distillery. Known for its rich heritage and craftsmanship, Blanton's has become a sought-after favorite among bourbon enthusiasts.

Each bottle of Blanton's comes from a single barrel, resulting in unique flavor profiles that can vary from one release to another. Typically bottled at 93 proof, this bourbon is aged in charred American white oak barrels, allowing it to develop a complex character over time. 

On the nose, Blanton's Single Barrel offers inviting aromas of honey, caramel, and ripe fruit, complemented by hints of citrus and spice. The palate is full-bodied and vibrant, featuring flavors of cinnamon, vanilla, and dark chocolate. A subtle spiciness adds depth, while the finish is smooth and lingering, leaving behind notes of oak and sweet toffee.

Blanton's iconic packaging includes a distinctive bottle shape and a horse-and-jockey stopper that has become a collector's item in its own right. With its combination of quality, rarity, and craftsmanship, Blanton's Single Barrel remains a benchmark for bourbon aficionados and continues to capture the hearts of whiskey lovers around the world.

name: Oakley Spirits 20 Year Single Barrel First Release
description: The first release from Oakley Spirits, a brand inspired by the boundary breaking Annie Oakley, this 20 year old single barrel bourbon was sourced from an esteemed Kentucky distillery in Louisville. This single barrel was carefully nurtured by founder Molly Kingston who was able to secure stocks of well aged Kentucky bourbon, waiting until this barrel was at its absolute peak of maturity. This first release is being launched on Baxus in collaboration with Oakley spirits and Iridia. As part of this release Iridia has developed unique technology allowing each bottle to be embedded with its own digital molecular signatures. This industry leading anti-fraud will help to guarantee each individual bottle is legitimate.

name: Campari 1990s Bottling Liter
description: A circa 1990s bottling of Campari Italian aperitif in a 1 liter format. Older bottlings of Campari are particularly sought after for their use of Carmine, a red colouring derived from cochineal insects that gives Campari its iconic colour. In 2006 Campari removed this colouring for a non organic variant. The aperitif has also become notably sweeter in recent years and original recipe bottles such as this represent the brand at its true original peak.

name: Pyrat Cask 1623
description: It's name originating from the Old English spelling of Pirate, Pyrat 1623 Rum Cask lives up to its heritage, the experience an adventurous voyage across the sun-drenched Caribbean seas. Pyrat 1623 Rum Cask rests in a hand-blown and cork-stoppered glass bottle. It is created from a secretive blend of nine Caribbean pot-still rums and contains rums that even before this blending have been aging for more than four decades. Once blended it is aged further, resting inside hand-selected imported French Limousine and Toasted American sweet oak barrels. These precious casks bestow upon their content a complexity and intense flavor profile.

name: Cats Eye Obtainium 28 Year Bottled for Baxus - 2024
description:
Cat’s Eye Distillery is a small family-owned micro-distillery with a mission to craft truly epic tasting products and uniquely sourced items. John Baker, an owner, honed his distilling skills at Honey Creek Distillery in Van Buren County. Subsequently, extensive research and consultations with 22 micro-distillery owners in Colorado informed their approach. After thorough experimentation, the distillery introduced its inaugural whiskey, “Essence of Iowa,” followed by “Krupnik.”

A special release of the distillery's flagship Obtainium line, this expression was bottled for Baxus in honor of the 150th Kentucky Derby. To reflect that, the 28-year-old Canadian Whisky is bottled at a hearty 150 Proof.

name: Old Fitzgerald 9 Year Spring 2025 Release
description:
A 9 year old expression of wheated bourbon from Heaven Hill bottled as part of their highly sought after Old Fitzgerald decanter series. This Spring 2025 edition is bottled in bond at 100 proof.

# Old Fitzgerald

The origins of the Old Fitzgerald name are shrouded in mystery and marketing lore. The brand was first trademarked in 1870 by S.C. Herbst, a Wisconsin-based rectifier who reserved the Old Fitzgerald name for his finest barrels. Many of these were sourced from Kentucky distilleries—particularly those using pot stills, such as Old Taylor. Initially, Old Fitzgerald was marketed exclusively to private clubs and to first-class passengers on railroads and steamships.

In 1900, Herbst acquired the Old Judge Distillery in Franklin County, Kentucky, where he began producing his Old Fitzgerald whiskies. The distillery’s operations ceased during Prohibition, but the W.L. Weller Company later acquired the Old Fitzgerald name for bottling medicinal whiskey stocks. When Prohibition ended, W.L. Weller—led by Julian “Pappy” Van Winkle—built the Stitzel-Weller Distillery in Louisville, opening in 1935. Old Fitzgerald became its flagship brand, with pre-1939 bottlings containing sourced whiskey from pre-Prohibition stocks and later releases featuring the distillery’s own wheated mash bill bourbon.

Pappy Van Winkle insisted that every Old Fitzgerald expression meet the Bottled-in-Bond minimum of 100 proof, a standard that became part of the brand’s identity. Through the following decades, Old Fitzgerald earned its reputation as one of the most respected names in American whiskey. In 1972, the Stitzel-Weller Distillery was sold to Norton Simon, and as consumer demand for aged bourbon declined, the brand’s prominence faded. The distillery was closed in 1992 under United Distillers, which continued bottling remaining stocks before selling the Old Fitzgerald brand to Heaven Hill in 1999.

Under Heaven Hill’s ownership, Old Fitzgerald remained a quiet part of the portfolio until spring 2018, when the company launched the first Old Fitzgerald Bottled-in-Bond Decanter—an 11-year-old wheated bourbon presented in a glass decanter modeled after Stitzel-Weller’s original 1960s design. The series has since become a modern collectible, featuring age statements up to 19 years old.

Today, Old Fitzgerald stands as one of the most coveted names in bourbon, prized both for its historic Stitzel-Weller-era bottlings and its new Heaven Hill–produced releases. In 2025, Heaven Hill expanded the line with a 7-Year-Old Bottled-in-Bond expression, reaffirming Old Fitzgerald’s place as an accessible yet high-quality offering in the modern bourbon landscape.

name: Binder's Stash 9 Year Single Barrel #687
description: A 9 year old expression of single barrel bourbon produced in Bardstown, Kentucky. Barrel #687 was bottled by Binder's Stash, an NDP sourcing barrels. In June 2025 Willett announced a partnership with Binder's Stash and are reported to be sourcing their barrels from Willett's inventory. No source is declared on this barrel, but between the Willett partnership and the Bardstown noted sourcing there is a fair chance this is Willett distilled bourbon.

name: Rare Character Brook Hill 6 Year Single Barrel #627 "Soprano"
description:
A 6 year old expression of Kentucky bourbon sourced and bottled by Rare Character whiskey as part of their Brook Hill label. This single barrel named "Soprano" was bottled at 58.88% Abv with 148 bottles total produced. 

The Brook Hill label was originally the flagship brand of Paducah-based rectifiers Friedman, Keiler & Co. It was bottled before and during prohibition before finding its way to either Willett distillery or Heaven Hill as a Japanese export label. Rare Character acquired the label and now reserve Brook Hill for its finest, most spectacular barrels.

name: Old Sport Single Barrel #3-PA Straight American Whiskey
description: A single barrel expression of Old Sport straight American whiskey, a brand from Old Carter. The straight American whiskey for this brand is reportedly a blended whiskey with a mashbill of 99% corn and 1% malted barley before maturation in used bourbon barrels. Each single barrel is unique but yields have been remarkably low, with some offering under 60 bottles.

name: Colonel E.H. Taylor Jr. Small Batch
description:
A bottled in bond expression of bourbon from Buffalo Trace crafted from their low rye #1 mashbill.

# E.H. Taylor

E.H. Taylor was a figurehead throughout the 19th and 20th century whiskey industry, starting as a banker providing financing for distilleries and ultimately finding a love for their production. In 1862 young Edmund Taylor opened Gaines, Berry & Company with partners Hiram Berry and W.A. Gaines. Taylor toured Europe and learned whiskey making from the Scottish, Irish and Germans, discovering their use of copper in whisky production. In 1864 Oscar Pepper passed away, leaving the care of his distillery to his young James Pepper. Colonel Taylor had long been a friend of the Pepper's and was named guardian of James until he came of age, helping him to keep Old Oscar Pepper distillery alive. Taylor's firm would also open the Hermitage distillery in 1868.

In 1869 Taylor acquired the Leestown distillery, quickly renaming it the Old Fire Copper distillery. Then in 1870 he would walk away from his partnership with Hiram Berry and W.A. Gaines. In 1873 he tore down the Old Fire Copper distillery, setting about a large rebuild with brand new modern techniques like column stills, copper fermentation vats and warehouse heating much like the technology he saw during his European trips. Unfortunately this rebuilt distillery came at a high cost, and in 1879 Taylor was approaching bankruptcy. The liquor firm of Gregory and Stagg purchased his debts, forcing Taylor to sell the OFC distillery to them. Taylor was also forced to sell the Oscar Pepper distillery, resulting in James E. Pepper losing his company.

Gregory and Stagg kept Taylor on at the OFC distillery, eventually renaming the whole firm to E.H. Taylor Jr Company. In 1882 the OFC distillery was struck by lighting and burned to the ground, a travesty considering the investments Taylor made there resulted in his bankruptcy. The company rebuilt the distillery with Edmund's guidance, and this distillery is now known as Buffalo Trace, the iconic home of E.H. Taylor bourbon today. In 1882 E.H. Taylor Jr Company acquired the J. Swigert Taylor Distillery, a site operated by Edmund's son Jacob Swigert Taylor.

In 1885 E.H. Taylor had finally had enough, and negotiated the return of the J. Swigert Taylor distillery to his family in exchange for his remaining shares in the OFC distillery. He left Gregory and Stagg behind, reclaiming the Swigert distillery and renovating it. This is the iconic Old Taylor distillery, the site known today as Castle & Key. In 1887 he completed renovations, renaming it Old Taylor and expanding the iconic castle distillery. Edmund Taylor and his son Jacob would operate this site until the onset of prohibition closed them in 1917.

Taylor is sited as one of the main influences on the push for the 1897 bottled in bond act. Along with a number of other industry figureheads, Taylor sought to curb the blight of low quality rectifiers. These firms would blend whiskies, often adding potentially harmful chemicals to the spirit to make it go further. Taylor helped push through the BiB legislation, which guaranteed a minimum quality in bottled whiskey.

The Old Taylor distillery would be acquired by National Distillers in 1935 and reopened post prohibition. It was a well praised brand throughout the 20th century, but when National Distillers sold to Jim Beam in 1987, Beam chose to close Old Taylor. The site was sold a number of times but never reopened, but finally in 2014 Will Arvin and Wes Murry acquired the site and heavily invested. This is now the home of Castle & Key whiskey.

The Old Taylor brand was bottled by Jim Beam through the 1990s and some in the early 2000s, but in 2006 they sold the label to Buffalo Trace who have turned the E.H. Taylor line of bourbons into a highly allocated, highly sought after offering. The continued use of Taylor's name on bourbon for over 100 years has kept his story and legacy at the forefront of American whiskey.

# Buffalo Trace

Arguably the most prized distillery in the bourbon world, Buffalo Trace sits proudly in Frankfort, Kentucky. Distilling has taken place on this historic site since 1787, with warehouses and whiskey barrels stored there from the early 1800s onward. The property officially became a distillery in 1857 under Jacob Swigert. In 1869, Colonel Edmund H. Taylor Jr. acquired the site and renamed it the Old Fire Copper Distillery (O.F.C.).

By 1873, Taylor had demolished the old buildings and begun an ambitious rebuild, introducing modern innovations such as column stills, copper fermentation vats, and heated warehouses—technologies inspired by his visits to distilleries in Scotland, Ireland, and Germany. However, the renovation proved financially ruinous, and in December 1877, the St. Louis liquor firm Gregory & Stagg rescued him from bankruptcy.

The firm’s co-owner, George T. Stagg, took control of most of Taylor’s operations. Although Taylor remained at O.F.C., his stake was now minimal. In 1882, tragedy struck when lightning burned the distillery to the ground. Taylor assisted in the rebuild, but in 1885 Stagg bought out Taylor’s remaining shares in exchange for the Old Taylor Distillery. The O.F.C. distillery later changed hands in 1890, when the Duffy firm took over. Stagg retired from the board in 1892 and passed away in 1895.

A young Albert B. Blanton joined the company in 1897 at just sixteen years old. In 1904, the distillery was renamed the George T. Stagg Distillery in honor of its former owner. Blanton rose steadily through the ranks, becoming plant manager by 1917 and president by the time Prohibition loomed in 1920. Under his leadership, the distillery was one of the few granted a federal license to produce medicinal whiskey, which kept it afloat during the dry years.

In 1929, the U.S. government authorized six Kentucky distilleries to produce whiskey to replenish depleted stocks—George T. Stagg among them. That same year, The Schenley Products Company acquired the operation. Schenley would remain in control until 1982, when the distillery was sold to New York businessmen Freddie Falk and Robert Baranaska under their Age International firm. A decade later, in 1992, Japan’s Takara Shuzo acquired Age International and the George T. Stagg distillery—only to sell the distillery soon after to the Sazerac Company, while retaining the rights to the Ancient Age brand and its high-rye mash bills.

In June 1999, Sazerac officially renamed the site **Buffalo Trace Distillery**.

The following decades brought steady innovation and growing acclaim. Buffalo Trace experimented extensively with wood types, barrel treatments, and aging conditions. In 2002, they partnered with Julian Van Winkle to supply whiskey for his Van Winkle and Pappy brands. The launch of the Buffalo Trace Antique Collection, followed by the E.H. Taylor line, cemented the distillery’s reputation for excellence.

In recent years, surging global demand has pushed even core expressions into allocated status. Whether through its legendary partnership with the Van Winkles or its consistently award-winning limited releases, Buffalo Trace has become synonymous with craftsmanship, heritage, and the prestige of Kentucky bourbon at its finest.

name: Found North 8 Year Batch 010
description: The 10th batch from Found North, this cask strength American whiskey is blended from barrels sourced from Canada. This blend is comprised of 23 year corn whiskey, 19 year corn whiskey, 9 year barley and 8 year wheat whiskey. This blend was bottled at a cask strength of 61.9% Abv.

description: A single barrel expression of Indiana distilled bourbon matured for 7 years 3 months and finished in a madeira cask. This barrel was chosen by Rare Character exclusively for The Bourbon Thieves and is bottled at 55.48% Abv.

This single barrel was chosen by Pa BourBIN. The Reveries is a label and bottler founded by long time spirits reviewer T8KE.

description: A 9 year old expression of single barrel bourbon distilled in Indiana and bottled by The Reveries. Cask #2426, known as Chapter 3: Pebbles' Side Eye was bottled at 116.3 proof. This single barrel was chosen by Pa BourBIN. The Reveries is a label and bottler founded by long time spirits reviewer T8KE.

description: A 10 year old expression of single barrel bourbon distilled in Kentucky (rumoured to be Heaven Hill) and bottled by The Reveries. Cask #5022, known as Chapter 4: Dude, where's my sample? was bottled at 132.2 proof. This single barrel was chosen by Pa BourBIN. The Reveries is a label and bottler founded by long time spirits reviewer T8KE.

name: The Reveries 14 Year Single Barrel Bourbon #24115 The Prologue by PA BourBIN
description:
A 14 year old expression of single barrel bourbon distilled in Kentucky (sourced from a distillery in Frankort) and bottled by The Reveries. Cask #24115, known as  The Prologue by PA BourBIN, was bottled at 122 proof exclusively for PA BourBIN. The Reveries is a label and bottler founded by long time spirits reviewer T8KE.

This barrel was distilled in Frankfort from the notorious "BuffTurkey" mashbill. Produced from Wild Turkey's mashbill but distilled at Buffalo Trace. When Wild Turkey no longer needed these barrels, they were sold to the open market where NDP bottlers have snapped them up.

name: The Reveries 16 Year Single Barrel Bourbon Ten Years Of T8KE
description:
A huge 16 year old expression of Kentucky bourbon drawn from a single barrel. Bottled at a crazy hazmat strength of 140.8 proof, this barrel was chosen to celebrate ten years of online reviewer T8KE. T8KE is also the owner of The Reveries label/brand.

This barrel was distilled in Frankfort from the notorious "BuffTurkey" mashbill. Produced from Wild Turkey's mashbill but distilled at Buffalo Trace. When Wild Turkey no longer needed these barrels, they were sold to the open market where NDP bottlers have snapped them up.

name: Dream Spirits 11 Year Single Barrel Rye #529 For Bourbin Jawn Certified
description: An 11 year old single barrel expression of rye whiskey distilled in Indiana and bottled by Dream Spirits. This crazy hazmat strength rye is bottled at 70.3% Abv and was chosen exclusively for Bourbin Jawn Certified.

name: Bomberger's Declaration PFG 2025 Release
description: A 2025 expression of Bomberger's bourbon produced by Michter's as part of their Precision Fine Grain (PFG) series. This edition is finished in French oak barrels and bottled at 101 proof.

name: River Roots 14 Year Single Barrel Rye #XR-R-L1118 For Bourbin
description: A 14 year old single barrel expression of rye whiskey distilled in Indiana. This barrel was bottled at 57.91% Abv by River Roots barrel company exclusively for Bourbin.

name: River Roots 10 Year Single Barrel Bourbon #GE-B-B27 For Bourbin
description: A 10 year old expression of Kentucky distilled bourbon drawn from a single barrel. This barrel was bottled at 64.23% Abv by River Roots for Bourbin.

name: Colonel E.H. Taylor Jr. Single Barrel Store Pick
description: A bottled in bond expression of bourbon from Buffalo Trace crafted from their low rye #1 mashbill. This single barrel offering from the EH Taylor line celebrates the legendary Colonel Taylor. This single barrel was chosen exclusively for a store/group.

name: Found North 19 Year Ten Years Of T8KE
description:
A special blend of Canadian distilled whiskies from Found North, crafted exclusively for the online reviewer T8KE. 

This blend is crafted from 20 year rye whiskey, 22 year corn whiskey, 24 corn whiskey and 27 year whiskey. This was all married and finished in port pipes and peregrine casks. Bottled as a minimum 19 year old product at 63.2% Abv.

name: Found North 15 Year Goldfinch 1st Flight
description: A special blend of Canadian produced whiskies from Found North. This blend comprises of 15 year corn whiskey, 20 year corn whiskey, 22 year corn whiskey and 20 year rye whiskey. These barrels were married and then finished in Sauternes, Cognac and new wood before being bottled at 58.1% Abv.

name: Rare Character Brook Hill 6 Year Single Barrel #627 "Soprano"
description:
A 6 year old expression of Kentucky bourbon sourced and bottled by Rare Character whiskey as part of their Brook Hill label. This single barrel named "Soprano" was bottled at 58.88% Abv with 148 bottles total produced. 

The Brook Hill label was originally the flagship brand of Paducah-based rectifiers Friedman, Keiler & Co. It was bottled before and during prohibition before finding its way to either Willett distillery or Heaven Hill as a Japanese export label. Rare Character acquired the label and now reserve Brook Hill for its finest, most spectacular barrels.

name: Russell's Reserve 13 Year Batch 4
description: The 4th batch of Wild Turkey's hugely popular 13 year old age statement bottled in the Russell's Reserve series. Bottled at barrel proof, the 4th batch was released in Spring 2023. First introduced in 2021, the 13 year old expression has rapidly become a serious fan favourite and in 2021 esteemed writer Fred Minnick awarded it his American whiskey of the year.

name: Russell's Reserve 15 Year 2024 Release
description: One of the most sought after bourbons of 2024, this Russell's Reserve 15 year old has been crafted from barrels hand picked by Eddie Russell that were earmarked and carefully tasted throughout the last decade. Bottled 58.6% Abv, just slightly above their barrel entry proof of 115, this higher proof offering from the Russells was reportedly aged in Camp Nelson. Turkey fans will be familiar with this warehouse, with Camp Nelson known to be the location where some of the best barrels are stored.

name: Russell's Reserve Single Barrel For Baxus & BoozApp VIPs
description: A special single barrel of Russell's Reserve bourbon from Wild Turkey hand selected by the Boozapp and Baxus team on a trip to Kentucky. Barrel #23-0098 was distilled in 2014 and matured for 9 years on the 4th floor of rickhouse K at the Tyrone campus. This barrel was chosen by the team and a small number of selected VIP Boozapp users who had a sizeable amount of Russell's Reserve private barrels on their wishlist. As a spot on the pick opened up the Boozapp team flew these VIPs down to Kentucky for a once in a lifetime Wild Turkey experience. These Turkey experts helped pick the perfect barrel in collaboration with the third generation of the Russell family to work at the distillery, Bruce Russell. This 110 proof offering came from a particularly standout barrel Bruce had selected for the pick.

name: Dream Spirits 9 Year Single Barrel First Release
description:
A household name in the spirits industry, Prav Saraff's Dream Spirits aims to source the best possible barrels in order to bring quality whiskey to consumers. 

This is his first release which was matured for 9 years, sourced from an Indiana distillery and bottled at 57.4% Abv.

name: Beam's Choice 6 Year Genie Decanter 1964 Bottling
description: A 1964 bottling of Jim Beam matured for 6 years and bottled in a smoke green genie bottle decanter. This 1964 glass decanter was the same one used for the hit series 'I Dream Of Jeannie'. More info about the production history of the series and the bottles association can be found in this Forbes article: https://www.forbes.com/sites/robertanaas/2017/04/25/i-dream-of-jeannie-home-a-jim-beam-whiskey-bourbon-bottle-expected-to-fetch-100000-at-auction/
